module-1-course-enrollment-directory.label: Module 1 Project: Course Enrollment Directory
module-1-course-enrollment-directory.summary: Build a department-to-student enrollment directory through five cumulative multi-table steps.

step_1.title: Connect Courses to Departments
step_1.prompt: Start the module project. In step 1 of 5, Join the courses table to the departments table and return the course title along with the department name.
step_1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

step_1.solutionCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;

step_2.title: Add Enrollment Records
step_2.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 5, Extend the query to include enrollments so each result row represents one enrollment in a course.
step_2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

step_2.starterCode:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;
-- Project step 2: Add Enrollment Records
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Extend the query to include enrollments so each result row represents one enrollment in a course
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
step_2.solutionCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;

main_py.content:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;
-- Project step 2: Add Enrollment Records
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Extend the query to include enrollments so each result row represents one enrollment in a course
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;

step_3.title: Add Student Details
step_3.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 5, Extend the query to include student details such as name and grade level.
step_3.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

step_3.starterCode:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;
-- Project step 3: Add Student Details
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Extend the query to include student details such as name and grade level
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
step_3.solutionCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name, students.name, students.grade_level, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;

main_py.content:

step_4.title: Clarify the Directory Output
step_4.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 5, Use exact aliases for department, course, student, grade_level, and term without changing the row grain.
step_4.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

step_4.starterCode:

-- Project step 4: Clarify the Directory Output
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.

-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
step_4.solutionCode: SELECT departments.name AS department, courses.title AS course, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;

main_py.content:
SELECT courses.title, departments.name, students.name, students.grade_level, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
-- Project step 4: Clarify the Directory Output
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.

step_5.title: Sort the Directory for Review
step_5.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 5 of 5, Order the results by department, course, and student in ascending order for a clear review.
step_5.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

step_5.starterCode:
SELECT departments.name AS department, courses.title AS course, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;

step_5.solutionCode: SELECT departments.name AS department, courses.title AS course, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id ORDER BY department ASC, course ASC, student ASC;

main_py.content:

-- Project step 5: Sort the Directory for Review
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.

sketch-1.title: Project Overview: Course Enrollment Directory
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will take on the role of a reporting analyst tasked with creating a comprehensive course enrollment directory. This directory will connect departments, courses, enrollments, and students to provide a clear view of who is taking each course and which department offers it. The final deliverable will be a four-table directory that accurately reflects the relationships between these entities. You will build this directory step-by-step, ensuring each join is correctly implemented to maintain data integrity and readability.
